text: The article analyses a project of reform of Polish water law, focusing on the basic aims included in the new draft Water Law Act. It concerns the issues of: the structure of competences and tasks in water management sector after segmentation of tasks related to management of water infrastructure and water resources; shaping river basin approach in the framework of water policy and streamlining of financial rules. The analysis of discrepancies between current law and new amendments makes it possible to identify and explain the defects of the law, existing in practice. The conclusions sum up whether the proposed solutions will contribute to the elimination of these deficiencies, and provide an opportunity for better water resource management in Poland.
headline: Fully Human Written
